reasoning: Solid CBC padding oracle implementation elevated by a timing-based oracle (both error paths return identical messages, forcing the attacker to distinguish via the 20ms vs 100ms delay) rather than the trivial message-based variant the description incorrectly claims. The anti-automation layers — X-Integrity-Hash requirement, 5 req/s rate limit, and stealth mode jitter injection after 20 padding errors — add genuine solve complexity beyond textbook padding oracle scripts. Flag isolation is strong (build-time encryption, flag+init deleted, non-root user). Main weakness: the 80ms timing gap is generous even with jitter, and a patient attacker can trivially avoid stealth mode by pacing below 20 errors per 30 seconds.
